text.title: RealtimeVoiceAgent with VAD and Threading
text.summary: RealtimeVoiceAgent with VADConfig, threaded audio capture, energy-based VAD, and utterance processing.

section.title: Architecture
section.p: RealtimeVoiceAgent uses two threads: one captures audio from the microphone into a queue; the second processes chunks with energy-based VAD, accumulates speech, and dispatches complete utterances to the STT-LLM-TTS handler.

section.title: Energy-Based VAD
section.p: Energy-based VAD computes RMS of audio chunks and compares to a threshold. This is a prototype approximation — it fails in noisy environments, with background music, or HVAC noise. Replace with Silero VAD or WebRTC VAD for production.

example.note: silence_duration of 0.8s is aggressive for non-native speakers — users with longer thinking pauses are cut off mid-thought. Increase to 1.2-1.5s for diverse audiences.

rule.statement: Replace energy-based VAD with Silero VAD or WebRTC VAD before any production deployment in non-silent environments.
rule.rationale: Energy-based VAD triggers on HVAC noise, keyboard clicks, and background music. Silero VAD and WebRTC VAD use learned speech models and work reliably in real environments.

section.title: TTS Streaming
section.p: Stream TTS audio directly to pyaudio output as chunks arrive. Buffering the full response before playback adds 100-500ms latency proportional to response length.

example.code: def _speak(self, text: str): p = pyaudio.PyAudio() stream = p.open(format=pyaudio.paInt16, channels=1, rate=24000, output=True) with self.client.audio.speech.with_streaming_response.create( model="tts-1", voice=self.voice, input=text, response_format="pcm" ) as response: for chunk in response.iter_bytes(chunk_size=1024): stream.write(chunk) stream.stop_stream() stream.close() p.terminate()
